argument_catalog.title: The Master Argument: From Free Will to Religion
premises: A-000001: Human free will is necessarily presupposed by all rational agents.
A-000013: Liberty and property rights are prior normative axioms to any scientific conclusion.
C-000001: Liberty in its objective form is individual property rights.
C-000066: Natural law grounded only in subject-centered reason cannot withstand the state or the majority.
intermediate_steps: If free will exists, agents are responsible for their choices.
If agents are responsible for choices, property (beginning with the body) exists.
If property exists, its defense requires rights.
If rights are to be defensible against the state, majority, or power, they require a source beyond human convention.
Only a transcendent (divine) source of rights is independent of state or majority.
argument_catalog.conclusion: Individual property rights require a divine source. Therefore, religion (as the formal system of divinely-grounded rights) is necessary for liberty. True religion = the formal system of liberty.

argument_catalog.title: The Game-Theory Argument for Religion (Last-Round Problem)
premises: In any finite-horizon game, rational actors have an incentive to defect in the final round.
If the game of social cooperation is finite (ends at death), the last-round problem unravels all cooperation.
Without divine resurrection, the game is always finite for each actor.
intermediate_steps: If actors know the game ends at death, reverse induction collapses deterrence.
No purely secular deterrence can solve this: surveillance is finite, punishment is finite, law enforcement can be overwhelmed.
Only infinite accountability (resurrection) removes the last-round incentive to defect.
argument_catalog.conclusion: Liberty is unstable in a godless world. Resurrection is necessary for the long-run stability of liberty and property rights.

argument_catalog.title: The Gödel Argument for Religion as Open Formal System
premises: E-000003: Gödel proved that any sufficiently rich consistent formal system contains truths unprovable within it.
A-000012: Religion must be a consistent formal system.
Religion is 'sufficiently rich' (it addresses all of human conduct).
intermediate_steps: Any sufficiently rich consistent system is necessarily incomplete (Gödel's First Incompleteness Theorem).
This means: religion-as-formal-system will always have undecidable propositions.
This is not a defect; it proves that religion cannot be replaced by a merely human system (Hilbert's program fails for morality as well).
Human reason as the 'inward proof' and Imamate as 'outward proof' provide ongoing guidance within the open system.
argument_catalog.conclusion: Religion is properly understood as a consistent but incomplete formal system—not a closed deductive machine, but an open system that requires ongoing interpretation within its axioms.

argument_catalog.title: The Self-Refutation Argument Against Denial of Free Will
premises: To deny free will is to make a claim.
Making a claim presupposes the ability to choose to assert or not assert it.
Choosing to assert is itself an exercise of free will.
intermediate_steps: The act of denying free will is itself a free act.
A deterministic agent cannot make truth-claims; it can only produce outputs.
Therefore the denial of free will is self-refuting.
argument_catalog.conclusion: The denial of free will turns into its own proof. Free will cannot be coherently denied.

argument_catalog.title: The Argument for Minimal Axioms (Against Mysticism and Totalitarianism)
premises: A-000011: A valid formal system must have finite, minimal axioms.
Mysticism proliferates axioms (every mystic, sect, or state adds its own axioms).
Contradictory axiom sets produce inconsistent systems.
intermediate_steps: Proliferating axioms creates contradictions.
Contradictions allow any conclusion to be derived (ex falso quodlibet).
This gives totalitarian states the logical freedom to justify any action.
Tawhid (one God = one ultimate source) minimizes axioms to one meta-axiom.
argument_catalog.conclusion: Tawhid (monotheism) is the minimal-axiom solution. Any system with more ultimate authorities (dualism, trinity, polytheism, dialectical triads) is logically susceptible to totalitarianism.

argument_catalog.title: The Argument that Science Presupposes Liberty
premises: Science requires that participants engage in genuine dialogue.
Genuine dialogue requires treating participants as equals.
Treating participants as equals requires accepting that each possesses free will and choice.
Free will and choice are the foundations of liberty and property rights.
intermediate_steps: Therefore, the framework of liberty and property rights is logically prior to the framework of science.
No scientific conclusion can override the normative axioms it presupposes.
argument_catalog.conclusion: Liberty and property rights are prior to any scientific conclusion. Positivism that attempts to derive liberty from science inverts the logical order.

argument_catalog.title: The Argument Against Democracy as Foundation of Liberty
premises: Individual property rights are not subject to democratic vote.
Democracy allows majorities to vote to violate individual rights.
Any system where individual rights are negotiable is not a system of liberty.
intermediate_steps: If the majority votes to enslave the minority, democracy produces slavery.
Therefore democracy is neither necessary nor sufficient for liberty.
Liberty precedes and constrains democracy, not the other way around.
argument_catalog.conclusion: Democracy does not legitimize the choice of the devil. Individual property rights are pre-political, non-negotiable, and not subject to democratic vote.

argument_catalog.title: The Argument from Ghadir: Wilayah is not State Power
premises: The Prophet's governance did not constitute a 'state' in the modern sense (no monopoly of violence over a territory).
What was transferred at Ghadir was wilayah (guardianship), not a political monopoly.
Those who denied Ghadir were seeking to re-establish the state.
intermediate_steps: Therefore Ghadir is properly understood as a religious-institutional transfer, not a political-state succession.
Confusing wilayah with caliphate/state is the source of the Islamic world's statist deviation.
The Imamate preserved religion against state capture; this is its institutional function.
argument_catalog.conclusion: Religion is the antithesis of the state. The Imamate exists to preserve religion against state confiscation, not to itself become a state.

argument_catalog.title: The Argument that Mahdism Completes the Theory of Liberty
premises: A consistent theory of liberty requires that liberty is not a permanent exception but a trajectory.
A society with a consistent theory of liberty will gradually recede the state.
The full receding of the state requires a condition where all human beings voluntarily accept liberty's axioms.
intermediate_steps: In historical time, perfect liberty cannot be achieved because coercive actors always exist.
The doctrine of the Advent (Mahdism) posits a future state where the cycle of domination is finally broken.
Without this eschatological horizon, the theory has no terminal condition—it remains an asymptotic trajectory without a limit.
argument_catalog.conclusion: Mahdism is logically necessary for the completion of the theory of liberty. Without it, the theory is an open-ended process without closure.

argument_catalog.title: The Argument Against the Austrian School's Incompleteness
premises: The Austrian School (Mises, Hayek, Rothbard) correctly identifies individual property rights and spontaneous order.
However, it grounds these in natural law or praxeology without a transcendent source.
Without transcendent grounding, rights are subject to state override, majority vote, or evolutionary revision.
intermediate_steps: Rothbard's four theoretical gaps: (1) grounding of natural law, (2) problem of last-round defection, (3) inability to explain religion's civilizational function, (4) incomplete theory of money.
Each gap requires theological grounding to close.
The theory of liberty extends the Austrian School by providing the missing theological foundation.
argument_catalog.conclusion: The Austrian School is correct but incomplete. The theory of liberty completes it by providing the theological grounding for natural rights.

argument_catalog.title: The AI Ethics Argument
premises: Artificial intelligence requires a finite, non-contradictory, executable ethical system.
No existing secular ethical system (utilitarianism, deontology, social contract) can be rendered as a consistent formal axiomatic system.
The theory of liberty claims to be a consistent formal axiomatic system grounded in religion.
intermediate_steps: If a consistent formal ethical system can be derived from religion, it can be programmed.
If it can be programmed, AI can be aligned to it.
If no consistent system exists, AI will default to serving whoever controls it (totalitarian states).
argument_catalog.conclusion: The theory of liberty (religion as consistent formal system) is the only existing solution for AI alignment. The challenge to any alternative: produce a consistent axiomatic system. If you cannot, you cannot object to this theory.

argument_catalog.title: The Mysticism-to-Totalitarianism Pipeline
premises: Mysticism separates humans from property, reason, and individual responsibility.
Without individual responsibility, collective entities (state, party, nation, class) fill the void.
Collective entities require a central authority to define and enforce collective identity.
Central authority with undefined mandate = totalitarianism.
intermediate_steps: Hegel's dialectic is the modern philosophical form of mysticism.
Marx applied Hegel's dialectic to class struggle → communism.
Fascism applied it to nation/race.
All share the structure: dissolve individual in collective → empower state.
Iran risk: if reaction to statist religion reproduces European post-Church mystical revival, same trajectory follows.
argument_catalog.conclusion: Mysticism (in any form) is the philosophical pipeline to totalitarianism. The only defense is a formal system grounded in individual property rights.
